senses.definition: The plural of bise, a musical or art term meaning a brief kiss or two small kisses on the cheeks (often used in some languages or poetic contexts).
examples: The two bises at the start of the ceremony were light and affectionate.
In the festival, friends exchanged warm bises on the cheeks.

senses.definition: A small set of two items; a pair or couple.
examples: The biset of tools includes a hammer and a wrench.
He bought a biset of cups for the picnic.

senses.definition: Describing something with two sets or two groups; occurring in two parts.
examples: The bisetose arrangement showed two distinct clusters.
A bisetose pattern appeared in the design.

senses.definition: Containing two sets or related to two sides; double.
examples: The bisetous nature of the project requires two teams.
A bisetous relationship exists between these factors.

senses.definition: Having two sexual orientations acknowledged; in modern usage, referring to bisexual.
examples: She is bisexed and comfortable with her identity.
Bisexed individuals may experience a range of attractions.

senses.definition: Relating to the number six; twice six; sixth.
examples: A bisext order refers to a six-part division.
In botanical terms, a bisext arrangement can describe a sixfold symmetry.

senses.definition: Attracted to both men and women; capable of having romantic or sexual attraction to more than one gender.
examples: She identifies as bisexual.
Bisexual people may face similar challenges as other LGBTQ+ groups.

senses.definition: A person who is attracted to both men and women.
examples: The club welcomed bisexuals and allies alike.
Bisexuals often navigate dating with awareness of multiple communities.

senses.definition: The belief or condition of being bisexual; a sexual orientation involving attraction to more than one gender.
examples: Bi-sexualism has a long history in many cultures.
Some discuss bisexualism as part of LGBTQ+ equality debates.

senses.definition: Plural form of bisexualism; multiple theories or expressions of bisexual orientation.
examples: Scholars discuss various bisexualisms across cultures.
The seminar covered different bisexualisms in history.

senses.definition: The range and experiences of bisexual attractions and identities among people.
examples: Researchers study bisexualities to understand diverse sexual identities.
Bisexualities include a spectrum of attractions and experiences.

senses.definition: The state or quality of being attracted to more than one gender.
examples: Bisexuality challenges simple labels about love and desire.
The program supports education about bisexuality.

senses.definition: In a bisexual manner; with attraction to more than one gender.
examples: She dates people bisexually, regardless of gender.
The author writes bisexually about love and desire.

senses.definition: People who are attracted to more than one gender.
examples: Bisexuals can be part of multiple communities.
The event welcomed bisexuals and allies.

senses.definition: Possessing sexuality or attraction to two or more genders; bisexual.
examples: The model described a bisexuous appeal in her work.
Bisexuous themes appear in the novel.

senses.definition: A chemical compound named bisglyoxaline; used in chemistry contexts.
examples: The reaction used bisglyoxaline as a ligand.
Researchers synthesized bisglyoxaline derivatives.

senses.definition: A mild exclamation used to express surprise, emphasis, or scolding; often a softened form of a stronger word.
examples: Bish! I forgot my keys again.
Bish, that was close—almost missed the bus.

senses.definition: A proper noun or fictional name; no common meaning in standard English.
examples: Bishareen appears as a character in the story.
The village of Bishareen is described in the map.

senses.definition: A proper noun or anthropological term; not common in standard English.
examples: The term bishari is used in the ethnographic text.
Researchers noted bishari traditions.

senses.definition: A proper noun; may refer to a historical or fictional group or person.
examples: The bishopar in the novel is a suspicious figure.
Bisharin legends describe a siege.

senses.definition: A plural form of a proper noun or nickname; not a standard English word.
examples: The team met the Bishes after the game.
Legends about the Bishes spread quickly.

senses.definition: A senior church official in many Christian churches; also a member of the clergy.
examples: The bishop gave a moving sermon.
A bishop is often assisted by priests and deacons.

senses.definition: A chess piece that moves diagonally any number of squares.
examples: The bishop controls long diagonals.
If you lose a bishop early, the game becomes harder.

senses.definition: A bird species; a common name for a type of bishop bird in some regions.
examples: The bishopbird nests in the savanna.
Birdwatchers photographed a bishopbird near water.

senses.definition: Plural of bishopbird; multiple birds of the bishopbird species.
examples: Several bishopbirds were seen at the reservoir.
The flock of bishopbirds moved quickly south.

senses.definition: The domain or jurisdiction of a bishop; the office or residence of a bishop.
examples: The bishopdom extends over several parishes.
He served in the bishopdom for many years.

senses.definition: Plural of bishopdom; multiple dioceses or bishoprics.
examples: The bishopdoms in the country coordinated projects.
Several bishopdoms voted to merge resources.

senses.definition: In general use, nothing; zero; a value indicating emptiness or the absence of something.
examples: The result was a null value in the dataset.
The experiment produced null results.
